text:
The teammate of a Dutch Olympian previously convicted of raping a 12-year-old girl has claimed that the Netherlands' beach volleyball duo are "making the best" of their time at the Games. Steven van de Velde's inclusion in the Netherlands team for the Paris spectacle has sparked major controversy due to his 2016 conviction.

The-29 year-old athlete served 13 months of his four-year prison sentence, leading to widespread questioning of the decision to allow him to compete in the French capital this summer. Van de Velde has chosen not to engage with the media during the Games and is staying in separate accommodation away from the Olympic Village.

His participation has been met with boos and jeers throughout his time competing in Paris. Despite suffering two losses at the tournament so far, including a 2-0 defeat to Norway, and recording just one win, the Dutch have advanced to the last 16.

Now, Van de Velde's teammate Matthew Immers has shared insights into the mood within the Netherlands camp, stating: "We're making the best of it, and we're really happy at the end of the match.

Follow us on Xfor the best and latest in sports news



"So we said to each other, 'OK, did we enjoy this moment?'. We knew before the match we gave everything and we said to each other, 'Yes, we gave everything'. And that's what we want to do every game, give everything and play our game."

"We lost the match, but we're second in the pool," he said, highlighting the team's standing at the Games as they prepare for the last 16. "It was against the Norwegians, so we lost close. But overall, I think we played really good."

After being convicted of rape in England at age 19, Van de Velde received a four-year prison sentence - but he only served 13 months before being released. He served time in the Netherlands following a transfer and has been back in competitive beach volleyball since 2017.

International Olympic Committee spokesperson Mark Adams previously addressed the decision to allow Van de Velde to compete, stating: "To characterise it as comfortable and happy would not be correct.

"We have made it clear we have been in long conversations with the Dutch National Olympic Committee. A crime occurred 10 years ago, a great deal of rehabilitation has taken place and strong safeguarding is in place."

Adams also noted: "The athlete is not even staying in village. We feel the NOC have explained their decision. Comfortable and happy, characterise it how you want, but the statement that they have given to us is correct and we will continue with the situation as it is."

title: Teammate of rapist Dutch Olympian speaks out on his state of mind after being booed | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Beattie
description: Steven van de Velde has been the subject of widespread criticism over his involvement in the Olympics, but his Dutch teammate claims the pair are still 'happy'.
